title: NOHARM: Medical Safety Benchmark and Randomized Study of Physician-AI Teaming
authors: David Wu, Ethan Goh, Adam Rodman, Jonathan H. Chen, et al.
tools_evaluated: 20 LLMs + 4 RAG clinical AI tools (incl. OpenEvidence)
key_findings: 24.6% severe harm potential from AI recommendations
Omission errors >80% of severe errors
Clinical AI tools outperformed generalist LLMs
Multi-agent AI teaming improved performance
AI-assisted physicians improved vs conventional resources
Combined human-AI would outperform both if recommendations fully incorporated

title: Generalist LLMs Outperform Clinical Tools on Medical Benchmarks
tools_evaluated: OpenEvidence, UpToDate Expert AI, GPT-5, Gemini 3 Pro, Claude Sonnet 4.5
key_findings: GPT-5 highest scores across all stages
OpenEvidence/UpToDate showed deficits in completeness, communication, context awareness, safety
Frontier LLMs outperformed specialized clinical tools

title: Expert Evaluation of Clinical AI Tools on Real Point-of-Care Clinical Queries (Real-POCQi)
tools_evaluated: OpenEvidence, Claude Opus 4.8, Gemini 3.1 Pro, GPT-5.5
key_findings: OpenEvidence outperformed frontier LLMs on real-world clinical questions
Contradicts June 2026 Nature Medicine paper
149 physicians in specialty-matched comparisons vs 12 in earlier study
Uses real queries from OpenEvidence platform

title: CSEDB: Clinical Safety-Effectiveness Dual-Track Benchmark for Medical LLMs
key_findings: Average LLM performance 57.2% — moderate
Safety 54.7%, effectiveness 62.3%
13.3% performance drop in high-risk scenarios
Medical LLMs outperformed general models by 15.3% overall, 19.8% in safety

title: MedCheck: Beyond the Leaderboard — Rethinking Medical Benchmarks for LLMs
key_findings: Widespread data contamination risks
Disconnect from real clinical practice
Neglect of safety-critical dimensions (robustness, uncertainty)
75% of benchmarks focus on clinical workflow

title: 11-System Blinded Comparison for Periodontal Disease Diagnosis and Management
scale: Periodontal disease scenarios, blinded expert rating
key_findings: 11 systems compared in largest dental AI benchmark

title: Blinded Multicenter Benchmarking: Specialized vs General-Purpose AI in Breast Cancer Care
tools_evaluated: Specialized medical AI vs general-purpose LLM
key_findings: Blinded multicenter comparison in oncology

title: Large-Scale Evaluation of 5 LLMs in Anesthesia Decision-Making
key_findings: 5 LLMs evaluated on anesthesia decisions

title: ChatGPT vs Gemini vs OpenEvidence in OB/GYN Clinical Decisions
key_findings: Three major AI tools compared in OB/GYN

title: Prescribing Caution: Critique of OpenEvidence for Medication-Related Questions
key_findings: Identified gaps in OpenEvidence medication recommendations

title: OpenEvidence vs AAOS Clinical Practice Guidelines for ACL Reconstruction
key_findings: Assesses OpenEvidence adherence to AAOS guidelines

title: 4 AI Platforms for Infection Control Across 3 Regions
journal: J Assoc Med Microbiol Infect Dis Can
key_findings: 4 platforms evaluated across 3 regions

title: Reproducibility of AI-Generated Antibiotic Recommendations in Standardized Scenarios
key_findings: Tests reproducibility of AI antibiotic recommendations

title: AI Accuracy for Disorders of Gut-Brain Interaction
key_findings: Evaluates AI accuracy for gut-brain disorders

title: LLM Systems and Prompt Engineering for Internal Medicine Pharmacy Cases
key_findings: Compares LLM systems and prompt engineering for pharmacy
